memberdef.definition: using DiscretePoloidalCSSplineMapping< X, Y, SplineEvaluator, R, Theta, MemorySpace >::BSplineR = typename SplineEvaluator::bsplines_type1
memberdef.name: BSplineR

briefdescription.para: Indicate the bspline type of the first logical dimension.

memberdef.definition: using DiscretePoloidalCSSplineMapping< X, Y, SplineEvaluator, R, Theta, MemorySpace >::BSplineTheta = typename SplineEvaluator::bsplines_type2
memberdef.name: BSplineTheta

briefdescription.para: Indicate the bspline type of the second logical dimension.

memberdef.definition: using DiscretePoloidalCSSplineMapping< X, Y, SplineEvaluator, R, Theta, MemorySpace >::cartesian_tag_x = X
memberdef.name: cartesian_tag_x

briefdescription.para: Indicate the first physical coordinate.

memberdef.definition: using DiscretePoloidalCSSplineMapping< X, Y, SplineEvaluator, R, Theta, MemorySpace >::cartesian_tag_y = Y
memberdef.name: cartesian_tag_y

briefdescription.para: Indicate the second physical coordinate.

memberdef.definition: using DiscretePoloidalCSSplineMapping< X, Y, SplineEvaluator, R, Theta, MemorySpace >::curvilinear_tag_r = R
memberdef.name: curvilinear_tag_r

briefdescription.para: Indicate the first logical coordinate.

memberdef.definition: using DiscretePoloidalCSSplineMapping< X, Y, SplineEvaluator, R, Theta, MemorySpace >::curvilinear_tag_theta = Theta
memberdef.name: curvilinear_tag_theta

briefdescription.para: Indicate the second logical coordinate.

memberdef.definition: using DiscretePoloidalCSSplineMapping< X, Y, SplineEvaluator, R, Theta, MemorySpace >::CoordArg = Coord<R, Theta>
memberdef.name: CoordArg

briefdescription.para: The type of the argument of the function described by this mapping.

memberdef.definition: using DiscretePoloidalCSSplineMapping< X, Y, SplineEvaluator, R, Theta, MemorySpace >::CoordResult = Coord<X, Y>
memberdef.name: CoordResult

briefdescription.para: The type of the result of the function described by this mapping.

memberdef.definition: using DiscretePoloidalCSSplineMapping< X, Y, SplineEvaluator, R, Theta, MemorySpace >::CoordJacobian = CoordArg
memberdef.name: CoordJacobian

briefdescription.para: The type of the coordinate that can be used to evaluate the Jacobian of this mapping.

memberdef.definition: using DiscretePoloidalCSSplineMapping< X, Y, SplineEvaluator, R, Theta, MemorySpace >::X_cov = typename X::Dual
memberdef.name: X_cov

briefdescription.para: The covariant form of the first physical coordinate.

memberdef.definition: using DiscretePoloidalCSSplineMapping< X, Y, SplineEvaluator, R, Theta, MemorySpace >::Y_cov = typename Y::Dual
memberdef.name: Y_cov

briefdescription.para: The covariant form of the second physical coordinate.

memberdef.definition: using DiscretePoloidalCSSplineMapping< X, Y, SplineEvaluator, R, Theta, MemorySpace >::R_cov = typename R::Dual
memberdef.name: R_cov

briefdescription.para: The covariant form of the first logical coordinate.

memberdef.definition: using DiscretePoloidalCSSplineMapping< X, Y, SplineEvaluator, R, Theta, MemorySpace >::Theta_cov = typename Theta::Dual
memberdef.name: Theta_cov

briefdescription.para: The covariant form of the second logical coordinate.

memberdef.definition: using DiscretePoloidalCSSplineMapping< X, Y, SplineEvaluator, R, Theta, MemorySpace >::spline_idx_range = IdxRange<BSplineR, BSplineTheta>
memberdef.name: spline_idx_range

memberdef.definition: using DiscretePoloidalCSSplineMapping< X, Y, SplineEvaluator, R, Theta, MemorySpace >::SplineType = DConstField<spline_idx_range, MemorySpace>

memberdef.definition: using DiscretePoloidalCSSplineMapping< X, Y, SplineEvaluator, R, Theta, MemorySpace >::IdxRangeRTheta = typename SplineEvaluator::evaluation_domain_type
memberdef.name: IdxRangeRTheta

memberdef.definition: using DiscretePoloidalCSSplineMapping< X, Y, SplineEvaluator, R, Theta, MemorySpace >::IdxRangeTheta = typename SplineEvaluator::evaluation_domain_type2
memberdef.name: IdxRangeTheta

memberdef.definition: using DiscretePoloidalCSSplineMapping< X, Y, SplineEvaluator, R, Theta, MemorySpace >::IdxTheta = typename IdxRangeTheta::discrete_element_type
memberdef.name: IdxTheta

memberdef.definition: using DiscretePoloidalCSSplineMapping< X, Y, SplineEvaluator, R, Theta, MemorySpace >::IdxRTheta = typename IdxRangeRTheta::discrete_element_type
memberdef.name: IdxRTheta

memberdef.definition: using DiscretePoloidalCSSplineMapping< X, Y, SplineEvaluator, R, Theta, MemorySpace >::IdxStepRTheta = typename IdxRangeRTheta::discrete_vector_type
memberdef.name: IdxStepRTheta

memberdef.definition: SplineType DiscretePoloidalCSSplineMapping< X, Y, SplineEvaluator, R, Theta, MemorySpace >::m_x_spline_representation
memberdef.name: m_x_spline_representation

memberdef.definition: SplineType DiscretePoloidalCSSplineMapping< X, Y, SplineEvaluator, R, Theta, MemorySpace >::m_y_spline_representation
memberdef.name: m_y_spline_representation

memberdef.definition: SplineEvaluator DiscretePoloidalCSSplineMapping< X, Y, SplineEvaluator, R, Theta, MemorySpace >::m_spline_evaluator
memberdef.name: m_spline_evaluator

memberdef.definition: IdxRangeRTheta DiscretePoloidalCSSplineMapping< X, Y, SplineEvaluator, R, Theta, MemorySpace >::m_idx_range_singular_point
memberdef.name: m_idx_range_singular_point

memberdef.definition: Coord<X, Y> DiscretePoloidalCSSplineMapping< X, Y, SplineEvaluator, R, Theta, MemorySpace >::m_o_point
memberdef.name: m_o_point

memberdef.definition: DiscretePoloidalCSSplineMapping< X, Y, SplineEvaluator, R, Theta, MemorySpace >::DiscretePoloidalCSSplineMapping
memberdef.argsstring: (SplineType curvilinear_to_x, SplineType curvilinear_to_y, SplineEvaluator const &evaluator, IdxRangeRTheta idx_range_singular_point)
memberdef.name: DiscretePoloidalCSSplineMapping

briefdescription.para: Instantiate a DiscretePoloidalCSSplineMapping from the coefficients of 2D splines approximating the mapping.

detaileddescription.para: A discrete mapping is a mapping whose values are known only at the mesh points of the grid. To interpolate the mapping, we use B-splines. The DiscretePoloidalCSSplineMapping mapping is initialised from the coefficients in front of the basis splines which arise when we approximate the functions $ x(r,\theta) $, and $ y(r,\theta) $ (with $ x $ and $ y $ the physical dimensions in the logical domain) with Splines (using SplineBuilder2D). Then to interpolate the mapping, we will evaluate the decomposed functions on B-splines (see DiscretePoloidalCSSplineMapping::operator()).
Here, the evaluator is given as input.

parameterdescription.para: Bsplines coefficients of the first physical dimension in the logical domain.
Bsplines coefficients of the second physical dimension in the logical domain.
The evaluator used to evaluate the mapping.
The index range describing the points which should be used to evaluate functions at the central point.

memberdef.definition: KOKKOS_INLINE_FUNCTION Coord< X, Y > DiscretePoloidalCSSplineMapping< X, Y, SplineEvaluator, R, Theta, MemorySpace >::o_point
memberdef.name: o_point

briefdescription.para: Get the O-point in Cartesian coordinates.

detaileddescription.para: Get the O-point of this mapping in Cartesian coordinates. This is calculated in the constructor.

memberdef.definition: KOKKOS_FUNCTION Coord< X, Y > DiscretePoloidalCSSplineMapping< X, Y, SplineEvaluator, R, Theta, MemorySpace >::operator()
memberdef.name: operator()

briefdescription.para: Compute the physical coordinates from the logical coordinates.

detaileddescription.para: It evaluates the decomposed mapping on B-splines at the coordinate point with a SplineEvaluator2D.

parameterdescription.para: The coordinates in the logical domain.

simplesect.para: The coordinates of the mapping in the physical domain.

memberdef.definition: void DiscretePoloidalCSSplineMapping< X, Y, SplineEvaluator, R, Theta, MemorySpace >::operator()
memberdef.argsstring: (ExecSpace exec_space, Field< Coord< X, Y >, IdxRange< GridR, GridTheta > > coords)
memberdef.name: operator()

param.type: Field< Coord< X, Y >, IdxRange< GridR, GridTheta > >

briefdescription.para: Compute the physical coordinates at the points on the logical grid.

detaileddescription.para: It evaluates the decomposed mapping on B-splines at the coordinate point with a SplineEvaluator2D.

parameterdescription.para: The execution space where the calculation should be carried out.
The coordinates of the mapping in the physical domain at the grid points.

memberdef.type: KOKKOS_FUNCTION DTensor< VectorIndexSet< X, Y >, VectorIndexSet< R_cov, Theta_cov > >
memberdef.definition: KOKKOS_FUNCTION DTensor< VectorIndexSet< X, Y >, VectorIndexSet< R_cov, Theta_cov > > DiscretePoloidalCSSplineMapping< X, Y, SplineEvaluator, R, Theta, MemorySpace >::jacobian_matrix
memberdef.name: jacobian_matrix

briefdescription.para: Compute full Jacobian matrix.

detaileddescription.para: For some computations, we need the complete Jacobian matrix or just the coefficients. The coefficients can be given independently with the function jacobian_component.

parameterdescription.para: The coordinate where we evaluate the Jacobian matrix.

memberdef.definition: KOKKOS_INLINE_FUNCTION double DiscretePoloidalCSSplineMapping< X, Y, SplineEvaluator, R, Theta, MemorySpace >::jacobian_component
memberdef.name: jacobian_component

briefdescription.para: Compute the (i,j) coefficient of the Jacobian matrix.

detaileddescription.para: For a mapping given by $ \mathcal{F} : {q_i}\mapsto {x_i} $, with ${q_i}$ the curvilinear coordinates and ${x_i}$ the Cartesian coordinates, the (i,j) coefficient of the Jacobian matrix is given by $ J^i_j\frac{\partial x_i}{\partial q_j} $.

As the mapping is decomposed on B-splines, it means it computes the derivatives of B-splines $ \frac{\partial x_i}{\partial q_j} (r,\theta)= \sum_k c_{x_i,k} \frac{\partial B_k}{\partial q_j}(r,\theta)$ (the derivatives are implemented in SplineEvaluator2D).

parameterdescription.para: The coordinate where we evaluate the Jacobian matrix.

simplesect.para: A double with the value of the (i,j) coefficient of the Jacobian matrix.

memberdef.definition: KOKKOS_FUNCTION double DiscretePoloidalCSSplineMapping< X, Y, SplineEvaluator, R, Theta, MemorySpace >::jacobian
memberdef.name: jacobian

briefdescription.para: Compute the Jacobian, the determinant of the Jacobian matrix of the mapping.

parameterdescription.para: The coordinate where we evaluate the Jacobian.

simplesect.para: A double with the value of the determinant of the Jacobian matrix.

memberdef.type: KOKKOS_INLINE_FUNCTION DTensor< VectorIndexSet< X, Y >, VectorIndexSet< R_cov, Theta_cov > >
memberdef.definition: KOKKOS_INLINE_FUNCTION DTensor< VectorIndexSet< X, Y >, VectorIndexSet< R_cov, Theta_cov > > DiscretePoloidalCSSplineMapping< X, Y, SplineEvaluator, R, Theta, MemorySpace >::first_order_jacobian_matrix_r_rtheta
memberdef.name: first_order_jacobian_matrix_r_rtheta

briefdescription.para: Get the first order expansion of the Jacobian matrix with the theta component divided by r. The expansion is carried out around $ r=0 $. The returned matrix $ J$ is defined as: $ J_{00} = \frac{\partial x}{\partial r}(r, \theta) $ $ J_{01} = \frac{1}{r} \frac{\partial x}{\partial \theta}(r, \theta) + O(r^2) = \frac{\partial^2 x}{\partial r \partial \theta}(0, \theta) $ $ J_{10} = \frac{\partial y}{\partial r}(r, \theta) $ $ J_{11} = \frac{1}{r} \frac{\partial y}{\partial \theta}(r, \theta) + O(r^2) = \frac{\partial^2 y}{\partial r \partial \theta}(0, \theta) $.

parameterdescription.para: The coordinate where we evaluate the Jacobian.

simplesect.para: The first order expansion of the Jacobian matrix with the theta component divided by r.

memberdef.definition: KOKKOS_INLINE_FUNCTION IdxRangeRTheta DiscretePoloidalCSSplineMapping< X, Y, SplineEvaluator, R, Theta, MemorySpace >::idx_range_singular_point
memberdef.name: idx_range_singular_point

briefdescription.para: Get the index range describing the points which should be used to evaluate functions at the central point.

simplesect.para: An index range covering the O-point.

memberdef.definition: KOKKOS_INLINE_FUNCTION const Coord< X, Y > DiscretePoloidalCSSplineMapping< X, Y, SplineEvaluator, R, Theta, MemorySpace >::control_point
memberdef.name: control_point

briefdescription.para: Get a control point of the mapping on B-splines.

detaileddescription.para: The mapping $ (r,\theta) \mapsto (x,y) $ decomposed on B-splines can be identified by its control points $ \{(c_{x,k}, c_{y,k})\}_{k} $ where $ c_{x,k} $ and $ c_{y,k} $ are the B-splines coefficients:
$ x(r,\theta) = \sum_{k=0}^{N_r\times N_{\theta}-1} c_{x, k} B_k(r,\theta) $,
$ y(r,\theta) = \sum_{k=0}^{N_r\times N_{\theta}-1} c_{y, k} B_k(r,\theta) $,
where $ N_r\times N_{\theta} $ is the number of B-splines.

The control points can be obtained by interpolating the mapping on interpolation points (see GrevilleInterpolationPoints or KnotsAsInterpolationPoints). We can also note that the first control points $ \{(c_{x,k}, c_{y,k})\}_{k=0}^{N_{\theta}} $ are equal to the pole $ (c_{x,k}, c_{y,k}) = (x_0,y_0) , \ \forall k = 0, ..., N_{\theta}-1 $ where $ x(0,\theta), y(0,\theta) = (x_0,y_0) \ \forall \theta $.

parameterdescription.para: The number of the control point.

memberdef.definition: void DiscretePoloidalCSSplineMapping< X, Y, SplineEvaluator, R, Theta, MemorySpace >::control_points
memberdef.argsstring: (ExecSpace exec_space, Field< Coord< X, Y >, IdxRange< BSplineR, BSplineTheta >, MemorySpace > pts) const
memberdef.name: control_points

param.type: Field< Coord< X, Y >, IdxRange< BSplineR, BSplineTheta >, MemorySpace >

briefdescription.para: Get a set of control points of the mapping on B-splines.

detaileddescription.para: The mapping $ (r,\theta) \mapsto (x,y) $ decomposed on B-splines can be identified by its control points $ \{(c_{x,k}, c_{y,k})\}_{k} $ where $ c_{x,k} $ and $ c_{y,k} $ are the B-splines coefficients:
$ x(r,\theta) = \sum_{k=0}^{N_r\times N_{\theta}-1} c_{x, k} B_k(r,\theta) $,
$ y(r,\theta) = \sum_{k=0}^{N_r\times N_{\theta}-1} c_{y, k} B_k(r,\theta) $,
where $ N_r\times N_{\theta} $ is the number of B-splines.

The control points can be obtained by interpolating the mapping on interpolation points (see GrevilleInterpolationPoints or KnotsAsInterpolationPoints). We can also note that the first control points $ \{(c_{x,k}, c_{y,k})\}_{k=0}^{N_{\theta}} $ are equal to the pole $ (c_{x,k}, c_{y,k}) = (x_0,y_0) , \ \forall k = 0, ..., N_{\theta}-1 $ where $ x(0,\theta), y(0,\theta) = (x_0,y_0) \ \forall \theta $.

parameterdescription.para: The execution space where the calculations should be carried out.
The control points of the mapping.

briefdescription.para: A class for describing discrete 2D mappings from the logical domain to the physical domain.

detaileddescription.para: The mapping describe here is only defined on a grid. The DiscretePoloidalCSSplineMapping class decomposes the mapping on B-splines to evaluate it on the physical domain.
This mapping could be costly to inverse.
